title: Mitigate credential exposure in Windows environments with Boundary and Vault
summary: This blog addresses the remote access and security challenges that still plague many organizations with Windows environments, specifically issues with static credentials and overly broad network access. We will then focus on how you can address these challenges with Boundary and Vault. Configuration steps will also be provided for anyone who wishes to test this out.

Due to the lack of automation in place and the burden of manual rotation, these credentials often remain valid for months and sometimes years, destined to fall into the wrong hands.
2. Boundary will detect the connection and automatically send an API to Vault requesting a new dynamic credential with a limited lifespan (TTL).
3. Vault will use its LDAP secrets engine to request a new Windows user from the Windows AD DS server.

The lifespan of a session and a credential are customizable and determined by the administrator. Once a session expires, Boundary will terminate the session, and the user will need to initiate a new session. Similarly, w

Getting started  Want to set this up yourself? Below is a step-by-step guide to set up a proof-of-concept with Boundary, Vault, and Windows AD DS. For simplicity and brevity purposes, the steps provided may not include e
A Vault server (version 1.21+).  HCP Vault  is the easiest and quickest to get running.
A Boundary instance (version 0.21+).  HCP Boundary  is the easiest and quickest to get running.

sections.overview: This blog addresses the remote access and security challenges that still plague many organizations with Windows environments, specifically issues with static credentials and overly broad network access. We will then focus on how you can address these challenges with Boundary and Vault. Configuration steps will also be provided for anyone who wishes to test this out.
sections.technical_breakdown:
This blog addresses the remote access and security challenges that still plague many organizations with Windows environments, specifically issues with static credentials and overly broad network access.

While multi-factor authentication (MFA) and directory integrations have improved identity verification at login, many organizations still rely on an underlying credential model with static passwords that are often reused across sessions.

While VPNs provide secure access into a network, it is often much more complex to limit lateral movement throughout the network.

Organizations can use traditional firewalls, security groups, and network segmentation, which all control access based on IP addresses rather than a user’s unique identity.

Rather than granting broad network access, it provides access directly between a user and a target resource based on a user’s identity.
sections.impact:
When an authorized user initiates an RDP session using their preferred RDP client tool, Boundary can inject credentials resulting in a passwordless connection. A user who has already authenticated to Boundary initiates a new RDP session to a target machine using their preferred RDP client tool.  

 2. In many environments, users authenticate to Windows machines using: 

 Shared local administrator accounts 

 Long-lived domain accounts 

 Service accounts with static passwords 

 Manually provisioned privileged credentials 

 Due to the lack of automation in place and the burden of manual rotation, these credentials often remain valid for months and sometimes years, destined to fall into the wrong hands. Organizations can use traditional firewalls, security groups, and network segmentation, which all control access based on IP addresses rather than a user’s unique identity.

Classified impact band: medium. Affected layer signal: cloud — aws.
sections.resolution: Resolution detail may be partial in the RSS excerpt. Refer to the original write-up for timelines, mitigations, and verification steps.
sections.lessons_learned:
We will then focus on how you can address these challenges with Boundary and Vault.

While multi-factor authentication (MFA) and directory integrations have improved identity verification at login, many organizations still rely on an underlying credential model with static passwords that are often reused across sessions.

The broad access challenge with VPNs  As organizations seek to improve security posture, overly broad access needs to be considered.

A better model: access and credential management  IBM Boundary fundamentally changes the model by combining authentication and authorization onto a single platform.
sections.root_cause_highlight:
Likely root-cause theme: deployment failure. In many environments, users authenticate to Windows machines using: 

 Shared local administrator accounts 

 Long-lived domain accounts 

 Service accounts with static passwords 

 Manually provisioned privileged credentials 

 Due to the lack of automation in place and the burden of manual rotation, these credentials often remain valid for months and sometimes years, destined to fall into the wrong hands. Example output: 

 Outputs: 

Deployment_Status = <<EOT 
================================================================ 
DEPLOYMENT STARTED SUCCESSFULLY 
================================================================ 
Server IP: 100.95.220.189 
Domain: adldap.lab 
Subnet CIDR: 172.31.10.0/24 
Username: Administrator 
Password: H4fE9t_3U-e*ABCDJ>uH 
CA Cert: Saved to 'ca.pem' Configure Windows AD DS server   In this section, you will create a new user account ( vault-svc ) and two new groups ( vault-user  and  vault-svc-admin ) in Windows Active Directory. Create another new group from the Active Directory Administrative Center: 

 On left side panel, double-click on  adlap (local) 

 Right-click on  Users 

 Select  New 

 Select  Group 

 Note: The reason you are creating this group is because when a Boundary user connects to a Windows target in this domain, a temporary user account will be created in this group.

sections.trends: Large-scale outages remain the forcing function for resilient architecture, communication, and automation investments.
